description: Harmony is a batch correction tool. Upon use, this module will produce three scatterplots. One plot will show the data pre-Harmony, and can be found in the 'BeforeHarmonyPlot.png' file. In this plot, the x and y axes represent the 1st and 2nd principal components from PCA dimensional reduction (unless specified otherwise by the user). Another plot will show the data post-Harmony, and can be found in the 'AfterHarmonyPlot.png' file. In this plot, the x and y axes the same dimensions used in the pre-Harmony plot are shown, but adjusted by Harmony. Lastly, the third plot shows the pre-Harmony and post-Harmony plots side by side for comparison.
author: Justin Lee;GenePattern Team
plan:
## Harmony GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
Harmony is a GenePattern module that Harmony is a batch correction tool. Upon use, this module will produce three scatterplots. One plot will show the data pre-Harmony, and can be found in the 'BeforeHarmonyPlot.png' file. In this plot, the x and y axes represent the 1st and 2nd principal components from PCA dimensional reduction (unless specified otherwise by the user). Another plot will show the data post-Harmony, and can be found in the 'AfterHarmonyPlot.png' file. In this plot, the x and y axes the same dimensions used in the pre-Harmony plot are shown, but adjusted by Harmony. Lastly, the third plot shows the pre-Harmony and post-Harmony plots side by side for comparison.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 27 parameters:
- Required: Input.RDS.Files, Output.Name
- Optional: Data.Set.Labels, Group.Name, Colors, Animate, reduction, dims.use, theta, lambda, sigma, nclust, tau, block.size, max.iter.harmony, max.iter.cluster, stop.early.cluster, epsilon.cluster, stop.early.harmony, epsilon.harmony, plot_convergence, verbose, reference_values, reduction.save, assay.use, project.dim, animation.iterations

### 3. Command Line
```
Rscript /opt/genepatt/src/Harmony_Wrapper_Script.R --file_list <Input.RDS.Files> --output_file <Output.Name>  <Data.Set.Labels>  <Colors>  <reduction>  <dims.use>  <theta>  <lambda>  <sigma>  <nclust>  <tau>  <block.size>  <max.iter.harmony>  <max.iter.cluster>  <epsilon.cluster>  <epsilon.harmony>  <plot_convergence>  <verbose>  <reference_values>  <reduction.save>  <project.dim>  <assay.use> <stop.early.cluster>  <stop.early.harmony> <Group.Name> <Animate> <animation.iterations>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/harmony\:v1

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: Input.RDS.Files
parameters.description: List of two or more Seurat objects to read in. The objects must be in .rds files.

parameters.name: Output.Name
parameters.description: Name of the file to save the data to.

parameters.name: Data.Set.Labels
parameters.description: Labels of the datasets, separated by commas. These labels will assigned in the order of the input RDS files. The dataset names will be assigned to the file names by default.

parameters.name: Group.Name
parameters.description: The name of the group that you would like to color by during the visualization steps. Harmony will color by dataset by default.\nDefault\: dataset_name

parameters.name: Colors
parameters.description: The colors you want to use to label the groups specified by the "Group Name" parameter, separated by commas. If no colors are specified, then colors will be automatically assigned. Refer to this link to see the list of colors you can choose from\: https\://www.datanovia.com/en/blog/awesome-list-of-657-r-color-names/

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Whether or not to produce an animation of the Harmony iterations.

parameters.name: reduction
parameters.description: Name of dimension reduction to use. \nDefault\: pca

parameters.name: dims.use
parameters.description: Which PCA dimensions to use for Harmony. The PCA dimensions specified should be in numerical form and separated by commas. By default, use all

parameters.name: theta
parameters.description: Diversity clustering penalty parameter. Specify for each variable in group.by.vars. theta \= 0 does not encourage any diversity. Larger values of theta result in more diverse clusters.\nDefault\: 2

parameters.name: lambda
parameters.description: Ridge regression penalty parameter. Specify for each variable in group.by.vars. Lambda must be strictly positive. Smaller values result in more aggressive correction.\nDefault\: 1

parameters.name: sigma
parameters.description: Width of soft kmeans clusters. Sigma scales the distance from a cell to cluster centroids. Larger values of sigma result in cells assigned to more clusters. Smaller values of sigma make soft kmeans cluster approach hard clustering.\nDefault\: 0.1

parameters.name: nclust
parameters.description: Number of clusters in model. nclust \= 1 equivalent to simple linear regression.

parameters.name: tau
parameters.description: Protection against overclustering small datasets with large ones. tau is the expected number of cells per cluster.\nDefault\: 0

parameters.name: block.size
parameters.description: What proportion of cells to update during clustering. Between 0 to 1. Larger values may be faster but less accurate.\nDefault\: 0.05

parameters.name: max.iter.harmony
parameters.description: Maximum number of iterations that Harmony will run.\nDefault\: 10

parameters.name: max.iter.cluster
parameters.description: Maximum number of rounds to run clustering at each round of Harmony. \nDefault\: 20

parameters.name: stop.early.cluster
parameters.description: Whether or not to stop clustering early. If TRUE, then the convergence tolerance is specified by the epsilon cluster parameter.\nDefault\: TRUE

parameters.name: epsilon.cluster
parameters.description: Convergence tolerance for clustering round of Harmony. A higher value indicates less tolerance, while a lower value indicates more tolerance.\nDefault\: 0.00005

parameters.name: stop.early.harmony
parameters.description: Whether or not to stop harmony early. If TRUE, then the convergence tolerance is specified by the epsilon harmony parameter.\nDefault\: TRUE

parameters.name: epsilon.harmony
parameters.description: Convergence tolerance for Harmony. A higher value indicates less tolerance, while a lower value indicates more tolerance.\nDefault\: 0.0004

parameters.name: plot_convergence
parameters.description: Whether to print the convergence plot of the clustering objective function. TRUE to plot, FALSE to suppress. This can be useful for debugging.\nDefault\: FALSE

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Whether to print progress messages. TRUE to print, FALSE to suppress.\nDefault\: TRUE

parameters.name: reference_values
parameters.description: Defines reference dataset(s). Cells that have batch variables values matching reference_values will not be moved

parameters.name: reduction.save
parameters.description: Keyword to save Harmony reduction. Useful if you want to try Harmony with multiple parameters and save them as e.g. 'harmony_theta0', 'harmony_theta1', 'harmony_theta2'. \nDefault\: harmony

parameters.name: assay.use
parameters.description: Which assay to run PCA on if no PCA present?

parameters.name: project.dim
parameters.description: Project dimension reduction loadings. \nDefault\: TRUE

parameters.name: animation.iterations
parameters.description: Number of iterations to animate.
